title: Crème Brûlée
excerpt: Ingredients 6 large egg yolks (use the whites for meringues) 100 g castor sugar seeds of 1 vanilla pod or 10 ml vanilla essence 500 ml real, fresh cream 1 vanilla pod 4 – 6 spoons white or light brown sugar Method Whisk the egg yolks with half of the castor sugar until light and …
html:
Ingredients

  • 6 large egg yolks (use the whites for meringues)
  • 100 g castor sugar
  • seeds of 1 vanilla pod or 10 ml vanilla essence
  • 500 ml real, fresh cream
  • 1 vanilla pod
  • 4 – 6 spoons white or light brown sugar

Method

Whisk the egg yolks with half of the castor sugar until light and creamy.

Heat the cream to boiling point and remove from the heat.

Stir in the rest of the castor sugar until dissolved.

Pour a couple of larger spoons of the cream mixture into the egg mixture.

Stir quickly and thoroughly to prevent the eggs from cooking.

Repeat the process once more. Pour the rest of the cream mixture into the egg mixture and whisk quickly and thoroughly.

Pour the mixture through a sieve into a jug and fill 4 small heatproof serving dishes (ramekins) with it.

Arrange the dishes in a deep oven pan (or even a cake or bread pan) and fill the pan with boiling water, enough to come up about 3⁄4 of the way up the sides of the dishes.

Bake for 30 – 35 minutes at 150°C or until the custard mixture is almost firm. Remove the pan from the oven and set aside to cool.

Lift the puddings out of the water and let them stand in the fridge for a few hours to get nice and cold.

Just before serving, sprinkle a thin, even layer of white or light brown sugar over each pudding and caramelise the sugar layer with a blowtorch or for a few minutes under a very hot oven grill.

Let the puddings stand now so the sugar layer can turn crisp.

Serves 4 portions with fresh raspberries.

Easy!
